summary.intent: Audit all 14 agnostic input components for implementation correctness, proptest coverage, and spec conformance, while reorganizing the monolithic test files into per-component modules matching the existing overlay/utility layout.
summary.outcome: All 14 input components audited and verified; both test monoliths split into per-component files (spec_conformance: 274→15 files, proptest: 1,403→14 files); two new proptests added (checkbox_group, radio_group); one spec/impl drift fixed (radio_group error message), one a11y bug fixed at root (ars-i18n fallback % suffix), aria-errormessage rolled out to 4 remaining components, and 11 specs updated. All gates green; PR committed and pushed, closing Epic #220.

repo: Per-component test layout convention follows overlay/ and utility/ precedent: each component gets its own .rs file, shared helpers/strategies hoist into mod.rs.
cargo xci-fast runs --workspace --all-features, so tests that fail under default features (e.g., i18n-dependent data_display snapshots) still pass the real gate.
spec/foundation/03-accessibility.md lines 1210-1225 blesses the aria-errormessage + aria-describedby dual pattern as the workspace a11y convention.
Spec files use string effect names (PendingEffect::new("complete", ...)) corpus-wide, not typed Effect enums — modernizing one spec in isolation would create inconsistency.
xtask spec validate checks 340 files; compile-snippets checks 2,171 code blocks in markdown specs.
file_trigger has no state machine (stateless) and is intentionally skipped in proptests — documented in mod.rs.
Snapshots that change require a snapshot-reviewed PR label.
PRs that close Epic-level issues use Closes #NNN in the body, not the title.

code.finding: fallback_format (no-icu4x/no-web-intl path) never appended % suffix for Style::Percent, producing aria-valuetext="50" instead of "50%" in default-feature builds — a real a11y bug, not just a test issue.
Error message emitted role="alert" while the component's own spec (§1.7) and all 8 sibling input components mandate aria-live="polite".
Workspace convention explicitly blesses aria-errormessage alongside aria-describedby; checkbox/switch/text_field/textarea were deviating from this foundation contract.
§5 prose stated validation delegates entirely to a Field wrapper, but the impl sets aria-invalid + aria-errormessage on thumbs directly — spec was contradicting the impl.
1,403-line monolith contained 12 existing proptests + arbitraries for 12 components; checkbox_group and radio_group had no proptests at all.

workflow: Parallel Explore agents (14) dispatched in two batches of 7 for per-component drift audit — agent reports were often imprecise/excerpt-based and required direct grep/read verification before acting.
TDD snapshot approach: run tests first to confirm snapshot failures catch the new attribute, then regenerate — ensures snapshots are mutation-resistant.
Stash-based verification to distinguish pre-existing failures from introduced regressions: git stash → run failing tests → confirmed failures predated the work.
Post-implementation audit phase should check adapter specs and foundation contracts, not just direct component spec/impl pairs.
When a fallback code path produces semantically wrong output (not just cosmetically different), fixing the root cause is better than gating tests behind feature flags.

friction: 7 pre-existing data_display::meter and data_display::progress test failures at branch HEAD ("50%" vs "50") were unrelated to input work but appeared as blockers until confirmed pre-existing via stash.
Explore agents produced imprecise findings from excerpt-limited reads — every agent finding required manual grep/read verification before acting, adding significant overhead.
range-slider spec §5 prose actively contradicted the impl, requiring both code investigation and prose correction rather than a simple attribute addition.
Proptest monolith (1,403 lines) required multiple sequential Read calls with offset pagination to fully ingest.
cargo test only accepts one filter at a time — snapshot regeneration for 4 components required 4 separate commands instead of one.
open_items: pin-input spec still uses string effect names (PendingEffect::new("complete", ...)) rather than the typed Effect enum — this is a corpus-wide spec convention gap, not a pin-input-specific issue; deferred as a separate corpus-wide task.
Mutation testing was explicitly skipped per user decision — coverage of new proptests (checkbox_group, radio_group) and the new aria-errormessage unit assertions has not been mutation-verified.
Codex review loop was set up (task created) but its completion is not shown in the transcript — review threads may need addressing.

checkbox/switch/text_field/textarea previously lacked aria-errormessage in all-features CI runs too (not just fallback) — the fix is landed but no regression test existed before this session to catch future drift.
